overall_justification: This edition of Correio da Manhã features a prominent, lengthy letter explicitly advocating for an expansionist approach to the Caixa de Conversão. The letter's arguments center on using the Caixa for currency elasticity, supporting productive sectors, and facilitating trade, aligning perfectly with the expansionist perspective.  The author criticizes the orthodox approach of "resgate cego" (blind redemption) and champions the Caixa's potential to stimulate the economy, even suggesting it could function similarly to the Bank of England's issuance of notes independent of metallic backing.  The sheer length and detailed argumentation of this letter outweigh other shorter, less focused pieces in the edition.

supporting_evidence.reason_for_inclusion: This quote encapsulates the core of the expansionist argument, viewing the Caixa as a means to achieve currency elasticity and a broader monetary tool, similar to those used by major European powers. It advocates for going beyond the initial, limited scope of the Caixa.

supporting_evidence.reason_for_inclusion: This passage explicitly criticizes the orthodox approach of focusing solely on currency valorization through redemption, arguing that it harms productive sectors. It reinforces the expansionist focus on supporting the present economic needs of the country.

supporting_evidence.reason_for_inclusion: This section connects the Caixa de Conversão to the valorization of coffee, a key concern for expansionists. It highlights the potential for the Caixa to provide the necessary currency elasticity to support this crucial agricultural sector.
